stories.headline: Green Bay bets the ranch on Christian Watson — $110 million says he's the man
stories.read: Well saddle up, pardner: the Packers just handed Christian Watson a four-year, $110.5 million deal, the richest paper a receiver's ever signed. Folks in the saloon are hollerin' about the price, but you ain't payin' it — you're readin' the signal. With Doubs and Wicks ridden off, there's a whole pile of unclaimed throws in Green Bay, and the ranch just told you exactly who's gettin' that cut. The herd's still grabbin' him cheap from his laid-up days — that's a vein nobody else has struck yet.
stories.source: fresh off the wire at NFL.com

stories.headline: Garrett Wilson's back on the range — full gallop, no limp
stories.read: Remember when that knee took Wilson down in Week 7 and your playoff wagon lost a wheel? Forget it. He's a full participant at Jets camp, runnin' clean, no surgery needed, and the fellas in green say he looks like himself. Here's the play: the saloon's still pricin' him like damaged goods, and a healthy No. 1 critter at a busted-wing discount is exactly the gold this Gazette exists to pan. Stake that claim before the land grab heats up.
stories.source: the scouts trackin' camps over at Fantasy Points

stories.headline: Myles Garrett rides west to the Rams — lock up your quarterbacks, NFC
stories.read: Cleveland shipped the two-time top lawman to Los Angeles for Jared Verse and a saddlebag of picks, and now the Rams' defense is meaner than a rattler in July. If your outfit drafts a defense, L.A. just jumped near the front of that pecking order. The flip side, pardner: every NFC West offense — McCaffrey's bunch included — now faces that buzzsaw twice a year, so shade their showdowns accordin'ly.
stories.source: a rider brought it in from the ESPN trade tracker
